days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring Tsim Sha Tsui

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel 108
timeline.detail: Settle into Hotel 108, a boutique hotel close to Tsim Sha Tsui station. Freshen up before starting your city exploration.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel 108, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Victoria Harbour
timeline.detail: Stroll along the promenade at Victoria Harbour to take in stunning views of the skyline. It's perfect for some memorable photographs.
timeline.mapQuery: Victoria Harbour, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Jumbo Kingdom
timeline.detail: Enjoy a delightful seafood dinner at the famous Jumbo Kingdom floating restaurant. Booking ahead is recommended for a guaranteed table.
timeline.mapQuery: Jumbo Kingdom, Wong Chuk Hang, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Avenue of Stars Walk
timeline.detail: Continue your evening at Avenue of Stars, reminisce about Hong Kong’s film industry, and enjoy the nightly Symphony of Lights show.
timeline.mapQuery: Avenue of Stars, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Hotel
timeline.detail: Head back to your hotel for a good night's rest. Enjoy the night's view before going to bed, as the city lights are a sight to behold.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel 108, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Purchase an Octopus card at the airport for easy transport access throughout your stay.
The Symphony of Lights is best viewed from the waterfront — try to arrive early for a good spot.
Book your Jumbo Kingdom table at least a day in advance to avoid long waits.

days.title: Exploring Hong Kong Island
days.subtitle: Culture and Cuisine

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australia Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a delicious Hong Kong-style breakfast at this famous eatery known for its toast and milk tea.
timeline.mapQuery: Australia Dairy Company, Yau Ma Tei, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Man Mo Temple
timeline.detail: Explore the historic Man Mo Temple dedicated to the gods of literature and war. The incense-filled atmosphere makes for great photography.
timeline.mapQuery: Man Mo Temple, Sheung Wan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Taste some of the best wonton noodles in Hong Kong at Mak's Noodle. This small but popular eatery has been serving locals for decades.

timeline.title: Ride the Peak Tram
timeline.detail: Take the iconic Peak Tram to Victoria Peak. The views of the city from the top are breathtaking, especially the skyline against the harbor.

timeline.title: Explore The Peak Galleria
timeline.detail: Spend some leisure time exploring the shops and take in panoramic views of the city from The Peak Galleria. Perfect for casual browsing.
timeline.mapQuery: The Peak Galleria, Victoria Peak, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at The Peak Lookout
timeline.detail: Enjoy dinner with a view at The Peak Lookout, offering a mix of Western and Asian dishes in a stunning location.
timeline.mapQuery: The Peak Lookout, Victoria Peak, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Return to Tsim Sha Tsui
timeline.detail: Take the Peak Tram back down and then a quick MTR ride back to Tsim Sha Tsui for the night.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel 108, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Get your Peak Tram tickets ahead of time to skip the queues.
Try to visit Man Mo Temple early to avoid the crowds.
Pronounce 'wonton' correctly; locals appreciate the effort!

days.title: Local Flavors and Farewells
days.subtitle: Final Day in Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dim Sum at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Enjoy one last dim sum experience at Tim Ho Wan, the world’s cheapest Michelin-starred restaurant. Arrive early for a table.
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Explore Wong Tai Sin Temple, known for its fortune telling and beautiful architecture. It's a milder tourist attraction with lovely light for photos.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Wong Tai Sin, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Local Eatery
timeline.detail: Dine at a local eatery to try some unique Hong Kong street food before you head out. A mix of flavors awaits you!

timeline.title: Shopping at Ladies Market
timeline.detail: Spend your afternoon browsing through the Ladies Market for souvenirs and local handicrafts. Perfect spot for last-minute shopping.
timeline.mapQuery: Ladies Market, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Head to Airport
timeline.detail: Leave for the airport, ensuring you arrive for your flight on time. Reflect on the wonderful memories made in Hong Kong.

localTips: Try to pack light at the Ladies Market, bargaining is much appreciated!
Ensure you leave for the airport early; traffic can be congested.
Check your flight status before heading to the airport.

hotels.name: Hotel 108
hotels.note: Located in Tsim Sha Tsui, near MTR station.
hotels.address: 108, Granville Road, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Round trip from Penang to Hong Kong

budget.item: Dim Sum at Tim Ho Wan

budget.note: Known for its great view and seafood
For meals not included in itinerary

urgent.note: Book at least a day in advance for seafood dinner.
